original_abstract: We introduce a novel dataset designed to advance the state-of-the-art in object recognition by embedding it within the broader challenge of scene understanding. Rather than focusing on isolated items, we gathered images of complex, everyday scenes that depict common objects in their natural environments. To support precise localization, we labeled these objects using per-instance segmentations. The dataset features 91 object categories—all of which are easily recognizable by a four-year-old child—and encompasses 2.5 million labeled instances across 328,000 images. Building this resource required extensive crowdsourcing facilitated by innovative user interfaces tailored for category detection, instance spotting, and segmentation. We provide a comprehensive statistical comparison of our dataset against PASCAL, ImageNet, and SUN, and establish performance baselines for bounding box and segmentation tasks using a Deformable Parts Model.
humanized_abstract: We present a new dataset to advance the art of object classification. We include it as part of the larger problem of scene understanding. Our new dataset, Scene Understanding through Object Recogntion (SUOR), is a new dataset of scenes and their objects. Our training set has 8K training images of everyday scenes with 91 objects recognizable by a 4 year old, 2.5M annotated objects and 328K training images. We collected our training set with crowdsourcing techniques such as specialized user interfaces for object classification, object localization and object segmentation. We present the statistic comparison for our dataset with PASCAL, ImageNet and SUN datasets and baseline experiments using a deformable parts model for object localization and object segmentation tasks.
